---
title: "Single Location State Space"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(artfima)
library(fields) # for rdist + Matern 
library(MASS) # for mvrnorm
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
source("ArtfimaCausal.R")
set.seed(324798)
mu <- 3
sigma_W <- 0.5

A <- artsim(
  n = 300,
  d = 0.3,
  lambda = 2,
  phi = 0.45,
  theta = 0.3
)

A <- A + mu + rnorm(300, sd = sigma_W)

mat = matrix(A, nrow=1, ncol=length(A),
             byrow=TRUE)

```


# psi approximates: 

```{r}
model = artfima(mat[1,], arimaOrder = c(1,0,1))
# Only really comes into play when we have multi-locations
# coords <- matrix( c(0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 1), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE ) 
# m <- 15 # Set truncation 
# n_loc <- nrow(coords)
# D <- rdist(coords) # pairwise distances 
# Matérn parameters

# The innovation variance from your fitted model
sigma2_eta <- model$sigmaSq  # or whatever artfima() outputs 
range_eta <- 1 
nu_eta <- 1.5 # smoothness 
m = 20 # Truncation 
Sigma_eta <- matrix(sigma2_eta, 1, 1)
psi <- psi_artfima(
  m       = m,
  d       = model$dHat,
  lambda  = model$lambdaHat,
  ar      = model$ar,
  ma      = model$ma
)
```


# state space basics

```{r}
# Dimensions
n_loc <- nrow(mat)
p <- 1
state_dim = m + 1


# Observation noise
#TODO: this is a possible contribution I could add!!! Fereirra assumes this is 0 actually
sigma2_W <- sigma_W^2 # measurement error variance 
R <- diag(sigma2_W, n_loc)


# Observation equation: Y_t = G*X_t + M*beta + W_t
G <- matrix(psi[1:(m+1)], nrow = n_loc, ncol = m + 1)  # [psi_0, psi_1, ..., psi_m]
M <- matrix(1, nrow = n_loc, ncol = p) # Design matrix for mean
beta <- matrix(model$constant, nrow = p, ncol = 1) # intercept (can estimate this)


# State equation: X_t = F*X_{t-1} + H*eta_t
F <- matrix(0, state_dim, state_dim)
F[2:state_dim, 1:(state_dim - 1)] <- diag(state_dim - 1)

H <- matrix(0, state_dim, n_loc)
H[1:n_loc, 1:n_loc] <- diag(n_loc)
```

# Direction run through

```{r}
# Initial state
X_0 <- matrix(0, nrow = m + 1, ncol = 1)  # X_0|0 = 0
Omega_0 <- diag(1000, m + 1)  # P_0|0 = large initial uncertainty

# One-step ahead state prediction: X_1|0 = F*X_0|0 + H*eta_1
# But we don't observe eta_1 yet, so for prediction:
X_1_given_0 <- F %*% X_0  # E[X_1 | Y_0] = F*X_0|0 (since E[eta_1] = 0)

# One-step ahead observation prediction: Y_1|0
Y_1_given_0 <- G %*% X_1_given_0 + M %*% beta

# Prediction variance for X_1|0:
Omega_1_given_0 <- F %*% Omega_0 %*% t(F) + H %*% sigma2_eta %*% t(H)

# Prediction variance for Y_1|0:
sigma2_Y_1_given_0 <- G %*% Omega_1_given_0 %*% t(G) + sigma2_W
```


# Running the first instance
```{r}
# Initialize
X_0 <- matrix(0, nrow = m + 1, ncol = 1)
Omega_0 <- diag(sigma2_eta, m + 1)

# First prediction
pred_1 <- predict_step(X_0, Omega_0, F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta)

# Suppose we observe Y_1
Y_1_obs <- mat[1, 1]  # actual observation

# Update with observation
update_1 <- update_step(pred_1$X_pred, pred_1$Omega_pred, Y_1_obs, 
                        F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta, sigma2_W)

# Continue for next time step
pred_2 <- predict_step(update_1$X_updated, update_1$Omega_updated, 
                       F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta) 
```

Building a run

```{r}
# Initial Vectors
Y_hat = matrix(0, nrow=nrow(mat), ncol=ncol(mat)+1)
likelihood = 0

X_t <- matrix(0, nrow = m + 1, ncol = 1)
Omega_t <- diag(sigma2_eta, m + 1)

for (i in 1:ncol(mat)) {
  # given previous instances predict.
  pred = predict_step(X_t, Omega_t, F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta)
  # update values
  Y_hat[1,i] = pred$Y_pred
  X_t = pred$X_pred
  Omega_t = pred$Omega_pred
  
  # zzzz some time passes
  
  # Now you see the thing, so update our values
  Y_obs = mat[1,i]
  update = update_step(pred$X_pred, pred$Omega_pred, Y_obs, 
                        F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta, sigma2_W)
  # Update values
  X_t = update$X_updated
  Omega_t = update$Omega_updated
  likelihood= likelihood + update$likeli
  
}

pred = predict_step(X_t, Omega_t, F, H, sigma2_eta, G, M, beta)
# update values
Y_hat[1,i+1] = pred$Y_pred
X_t = pred$X_pred
Omega_t = pred$Omega_pred
likelihood
```

And plotting!

```{r}

df = data.frame(predictions = t(Y_hat), originals = t(cbind(mat,NA)))
# Create time index
df$time <- 1:nrow(df)

# Reshape for ggplot
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(predictions, originals), 
               names_to = "type", 
               values_to = "value")

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = time, y = value, color = type, linetype = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("originals" = "black", "predictions" = "red"),
                     labels = c("Observed", "Predicted")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("originals" = "solid", "predictions" = "dashed"),
                        labels = c("Observed", "Predicted")) +
  labs(x = "Time", y = "Value", 
       title = "ARTFIMA: One-Step-Ahead Predictions vs Observations",
       color = "", linetype = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```
